x.title:
Most people I talk to in India have 1 child. Some have 2.

When I tell them I have three, their first reaction is total
x.summary:
Most people I talk to in India have 1 child. Some have 2.

When I tell them I have three, their first reaction is total surprise followed by "well, accidents happen".

The main reason for one? Raising kids is just really hard, especially for nuclear families in cities.

x.title: When we go from GPT-5.0 -> GPT-5.1 -> ... -> GPT-5.5, the number incrementing goes with improvements in capabil
x.summary:
When we go from GPT-5.0 -> GPT-5.1 -> ... -> GPT-5.5, the number incrementing goes with improvements in capabilities and token efficiency (which translates to speed gains). With GPT-5.5 our best model yet.

A simple strategy that we would like to continue.

videos.title: Terence Tao on How AI Is Changing Mathematics
videos.summary:
Fields Medal recipient Terence Tao explains how AI is changing the way mathematicians work, making it easier to experiment, explore new ideas, and collaborate on difficult problems.
The video also features OpenAI Chief Research Officer Mark Chen, who discusses OpenAI's broader goal of building tools that help scientists and researchers achieve breakthroughs at scale.
Together, they explore how AI can expand what's possible in mathematics and science while preserving the creativity, curiosity, and discovery that drive human progress.
Terence Tao was interviewed at an OpenAI Forum event held in partnership with IPAM (the Institute for Pure and Applied Mathematics) in March 2026.
videos.author: OpenAI

videos.title: Builders Unscripted: Ep. 3 - Matias Castello, Product Leader at Alchemy
videos.summary:
Builders Unscripted spotlights the stories behind real projects and the mindset that makes them possible: you can just build things.

In this episode, Romain Huet, Head of Developer Experience, chats with Matias Castello, Product Lead at Alchemy. Matias shares how his team uses Codex for code review and product work, how he builds side projects with Codex App Server, and why he keeps rebuilding Snapcat as a personal eval for each new model.

Chapters:
00:00 Intro
00:58 Bringing Codex to work at Alchemy
01:51 Using Codex for code review
08:04 Building side projects with Codex
18:51 Codex App Server projects
24:01 Computer use, GPT-5.5, and Snapcat
28:24 Advice for builders
videos.author: OpenAI

videos.title: Windows Computer Use and mobile access for Codex
videos.summary:
Codex on Windows can now use your computer to work across desktop apps, while you step away from your desk. You can also now use your mobile phone to control the Codex running on your Windows machine.

See how to enable Computer Use in the Codex app, mention installed apps in a conversation, and connect your Windows device to the ChatGPT mobile app to monitor or start tasks on the go.

Chapters:
00:00 New features for Codex on Windows
00:29 Enable Computer Use
00:43 Let Codex work on your computer
01:08 Connect to Codex from ChatGPT mobile app
videos.author: OpenAI

Don Lincoln is a particle physicist at Fermilab who has spent decades working at the frontiers of high energy physics.
Thank you for listening ❤ Check out our sponsors: https://lexfridman.com/sponsors/ep497-sb
